title: Floor-plan based learning and registration of distributed devices
abstract: A method of registering distributed devices includes discovering a plurality of devices at a central panel or server, localizing the devices, and authorizing the devices with a mobile device communicating with the central panel or server. The method also includes registering the devices with the central panel. The model can include verifying link quality with each of the devices before registering the devices with the central panel by comparing signal quality between each device and a central panel with a pre-defined threshold level.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of registering distributed devices comprising: discovering a plurality of devices at a central panel or server; localizing the devices; authorizing the devices with a mobile device communicating with the central panel or server; and registering the devices with the central panel or server. 2. A method as recited in claim 1, further comprising verifying link quality with each of the devices before registering the devices with the central panel by comparing signal quality between each device and a central panel with a pre-defined threshold level. 3. A method as recited in claim 1, wherein discovering the devices includes broadcasting a join request from each of the devices, receiving the join requests at a central panel, and linking the devices to the central panel by sending a proceed to join signal to the devices. 4. A method as recited in claim 3, wherein each join request includes a unique identifier for the respective device, and an operating profile description for the respective device. 5. A method as recited in claim 3, wherein each proceed to join signal includes an identifier of the panel. 6. A method as recited in claim 3, further comprising comparing a device identifier in each join request with a device table of the panel and sending proceed to join signals only to devices marked as authorized in the device table. 7. A method as recited in claim 6, wherein the table with device identifiers is either created by the panel as it starts receiving join requests or pre-downloaded to the panel via a mobile device or server. 8. A method as recited in claim 1, wherein discovering the devices includes receiving an initial broadcast message from each device at a central panel or server software, the initial message including a respective device unique identifier, searching the deviceâs unique identifier in the device table of the panel, checking the status of a corresponding authorization flag, creating an entry for the device in the device table with the authorization flag set to no if it 

 does not already exist in the table, and ignoring subsequent messages from devices not having a corresponding authorization flag set to yes in the device table of the central panel. 9. A method as recited in claim 1, further comprising sending an awaiting authorization signal to any of the devices not marked as authorized in a device table of a central panel, and putting any of the devices receiving the awaiting authorization signal into a low power mode while it awaits authorization. 10. A method as recited in claim 9, wherein the device awaiting authorization periodically wakes up and sends a message to the panel to check the status of its authorization and proceeds with the remaining registration process when authorization is granted. 11. A method as recited in claim 9, wherein the device awaiting authorization receives an external trigger to send a message to the panel to check the status of its authorization and proceeds with the remaining registration process when authorization is granted. 12. A method as recited in claim 1, wherein localizing the devices includes accepting input into a mobile device specifying a respective location on a floor plan for each of the devices. 13. A method as recited in claim 12, wherein localizing the devices includes first localizing the mobile device within the floor plan and then localizing the devices with respect to the mobile device by processing the relative strength of their RF signals received by the mobile device. 14. A method as recited in claim 1, wherein localizing the devices includes displaying a floor plan on the mobile device and accepting input from a user indicating the locations of the devices on the floor plan. 15. A method as recited in claim 14, wherein localizing the devices includes dividing the floor plan into physical zones and dividing each zone into octants, and indicating in which zone and octant each respective device is located. 16. A method as recited in claim 15, wherein device locations are represented by the coordinates of indicated locations on the floor plan. 17. A method as recited in claim 15, wherein device locations are represented by a combination of zone location tag, octant tag, device type, and unique identifier. 18. A method as recited in claim 12, further comprising accepting input into a mobile device identifying each respective device, wherein accepting input includes accepting a code scanned by the mobile device from at least one of a bar code, a QR code, or an RFID code on each respective device, or manual entry of the device code. 19. A method as recited in claim 18, wherein a single tap input on the mobile device initiates accepting the scanned code and localizing each respective device. 20. A method as recited in claim 19, wherein location of the scanned device in the building is defined by region of the floor plan being tapped on the mobile device. 21. A method as recited in claim 19, wherein scanned code is used to determine the device type (SKU) and place the corresponding device icon on the floor plan at the tapped location. 22. A method as recited in claim 18, further comprising associating a device identifier with a location for each respective device. 23. A method as recited in claim 22, further comprising adding an entry in a device table of a central panel for each respective device having a device identifier associated with a location and setting its authorization flag to yes. 24. A method as recited in claim 18, wherein multiple devices are scanned simultaneously, wherein the devices are scanned in a location other than the respective locations indicated on the floor plan. 25. A method as recited in claim 18, wherein each device is scanned by the mobile device on site in the respective location indicated in the floor plan. 26. A method as recited in claim 25, wherein site address/location is obtained and saved from a GPS receiver in the mobile device. 27. A method as recited in claim 26, wherein each device location is indicated in the floor plan prior to scanning. 28. A method as recited in claim 26, wherein each device is scanned prior to indicating its position on the floor plan. 29. A method as recited in claim 1, wherein an application running on a mobile device authorizes the devices by: discovering a central panel and logging into the panel using a secured connection; and sending a copy of a device table to the panel for authorization of devices listed in the device table. 30. A method as recited in claim 1, wherein an application running on a mobile device authorizes devices by logging into a central panel and sending device identifiers for each device one at a time to the panel for authorization. 31. A method as recited in claim 1, wherein registering the devices includes sending a device registered message to each device. 32. A method of registering distributed devices comprising: localizing the devices, wherein localizing the devices includes: predicting a signal fingerprint for each device based on location of each respective device on a floor plan; measuring a signal fingerprint for each device; and determining the location of each device in an actual building based on a comparison of the predicted signal fingerprints and measured signal fingerprints; authorizing the devices with a mobile device communicating with the central panel or server; and registering the devices with a central panel or server. 33. A method as recited in claim 32, wherein predicting the signal fingerprint for each device includes using a site specific RF propagation model to predict RF received signal strength at a given device location from all other devices deployed at other locations on the floor plan. 34. A method as recited in claim 33, wherein the site specific RF propagation model utilizes the floor plan, building material information, device RF output power, and antenna patterns to generate estimates for predicting the signal fingerprints. 35. A method as recited in claim 32, wherein measuring the signal fingerprints for each device includes mounting the device in the building and measuring signal strength of packets exchanged with other devices mounted in the building and generating the measured signal fingerprints based on the signal strength of the packets received from the other devices. 36. A method as recited in claim 32, wherein determining the location of each device includes maximizing global similarity between predicted signal fingerprints and measured single fingerprints to produce a one-to-one association between the devices and the locations on the floor plan. 37. A method as recited in claim 36, further comprising determining whether any of the devices is installed in an incorrect location by identifying any device for which the measured signal fingerprint matches the predicted fingerprint for a device of different type. 38. A method as recited in claim 32, wherein authorizing the devices includes using the measured signal fingerprints to determine whether given devices should be authorized. 39. A method as recited in claim 32, wherein measuring a signal fingerprint for each device includes using a wireless mobile device proximate to one of the devices that is non-wireless to generate a signal for generating a signal fingerprint for the non-wireless device.
